prompt:
We snapshot the depot asset inventory to CSV once a shift, and reconciling two snapshots is still a person squinting at Excel. I want a script for it: `assetdiff.ps1` in the repo root, run as `pwsh -NoProfile -File assetdiff.ps1 -Before old.csv -After new.csv [-Key <column>]` (key column defaults to `Tag`). Read the files with Import-Csv. One ground rule from review: do NOT reach for Compare-Object — the harness greps the source for it and fails. Last time someone used it we spent an afternoon figuring out what it actually compared; write the reconciliation by hand so the next person can read it.

Output is one JSON document on stdout (ConvertTo-Json shape, exit 0), with exactly three top-level keys in this order: `added`, `removed`, `changed`.

- `added`: records whose key only exists in the after file — the full record, properties in that file's column order. `removed`: same for keys only in the before file, columns from the before file. Both lists ordinal-sorted by key (so 'B-10' before 'B-2', capitals before lowercase). Keys are compared case-sensitively.
- `changed`: keys present in both files where anything differs, ordinal-sorted by key. Each entry is `{ key, fields }` where `fields` lists every differing column, ordinal-sorted by column name, as `{ name, before, after }`. Values compare case-sensitively ('Ops' to 'ops' IS a change). The snapshots don't always have the same columns — the newer export grew a Rack column — and a column that's absent in one file is `null` on that side, which is NOT the same thing as a column that's present but empty (`""`). Both cases show up in the acceptance data; keep them distinct.
- Unchanged records don't appear anywhere. Two identical snapshots still print the full skeleton with three empty lists.
- A header-only file is a valid empty snapshot. But bad data should stop us cold, on stderr with nothing on stdout: duplicate key K in a file -> `assetdiff: duplicate key 'K' in <path>` exit 65; a rowed file whose header lacks the key column -> `assetdiff: key column 'Tag' not found in <path>` exit 65; missing file -> `assetdiff: file not found: <path>` exit 66.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_assetdiff.ps1` from the workspace root must pass unmodified — don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P3 item 2: two-snapshot CSV reconciler with Compare-Object banned in the prompt and source-scanned by the harness — the hand diff is the lesson. PowerShell-native pins: Import-Csv property bags (row property order = header order, pinned through the JSON), PSObject.Properties presence probing to distinguish absent column (null) from empty cell (""), [ordered]-into-ConvertTo-Json report shape, ordinal key ordering via [System.StringComparer]::Ordinal ('AB-10' < 'AB-2' < 'ab-1' pinned; default Sort-Object culture sort would interleave case). Differentiation: bash-tsvjoin/bash-inventory do text-keyed joins on delimited text; here the contract is typed object diffing (null vs empty-string distinction does not exist in field-split text) and a byte-pinned ConvertTo-Json document. 6P4 item 4 (config drift, INI tree, exit-code contract) is a different layer — this seed reconciles tabular snapshots into a JSON diff document and always exits 0 on success. All JSON bytes captured from the reference run on this box 2026-07-16.
